pattern: Two-coloring by traversal: a graph splits into two independent sets exactly when a breadth- or depth-first walk can paint each newly seen node the opposite color of its neighbor without ever hitting a conflict.
hints: Try to paint the nodes with two colors so that no edge joins two nodes of the same color; a conflict means it cannot be done.
Traverse each connected component, coloring an unvisited neighbor the opposite of the current node and failing the moment a neighbor already carries the same color.
Keep a color array initialized to unknown; for every uncolored node start a BFS/DFS, assign colors alternately, and return false if any edge connects two equally colored nodes.

edge_inputs.description: odd cycle of length three cannot be two-colored
even cycle of length four is bipartite
single isolated node is trivially bipartite
two disconnected components, one bad component fails the whole graph
triangle hidden among extra isolated nodes
long odd cycle of length five

stress.description: random graphs, often planted bipartite, sometimes with an odd cycle injected
stress.note: mixes clearly bipartite graphs with deliberately non-bipartite ones
